Expert Neeraj Dewan sees opportunities in largecap blue chips and PSU banks, while also noting value in private lenders. The recent correction may attract foreign institutional investors back to India, with metals remaining a strong play.

topStories.title: Flip-flop on COP: Congress's dig at government on withdrawing bid to host COP33
topStories.description: It also calls into question the Modi government's true commitment to take on more ambitious carbon mitigation goals in the short and medium term, Congress leader Jairam Ramesh says

topStories.title: Did Trump's officials threaten Catholic Church? Vatican cancels White House’s invitation to host Pope Leo XIV
topStories.description: The US government under Trump threatened Catholic Church due to which Vatican postponed White House’s invitation to host Pope Leo XIV indefinitely. The Vatican viewed US official's comments as intimidation, prompting a reevaluation of diplomatic relations.

topStories.title: Karnataka bypolls at 9 am: 9% polling in Davanagere South, 11.87% in Bagalkot
topStories.description: The polls were necessitated in Bagalkot and Davanagere South by the deaths of senior Congress MLAs H.Y. Meti and Shamanur Shivashankarappa respectively

topStories.title: Trump Rebukes NATO Over Iran After Meeting With Alliance's Chief
topStories.description: NATO allies refused to help Trump protect commercial ships in the Strait of Hormuz or let the US use their bases to attack Iran during the war.

topStories.title: ‘Lebanon not part of the deal?’ Conflicting claims puts Iran-US, Israel truce in danger - Here's who said what
topStories.description: The ceasefire between Iran and the US is deteriorating as tensions rise over Israeli strikes in Lebanon. Both nations had claimed victory after agreeing to a truce, but Israel's actions have led to threats of renewed hostilities from Iran and Hezbollah.

topStories.title: For Gulf states, Hormuz uncertainty casts shadow over fragile US-Iran truce
topStories.description: Experts say Gulf states wary US could agree to deal giving Iran leverage over strategic energy waterway.

topStories.title: Who Is 'Ketamine Queen'? Woman Behind Matthew Perry's Death Jailed For 15 Years
topStories.description: Born on July 22, 1983, in London, Jasveen Sangha is a British-American citizen of Punjabi origin. She studied in the US and even completed an MBA in London.

topStories.title: Russia offers South Asian countries sanctioned LNG at 40% discount amid war-induced energy crisis: Report
topStories.description: While Russia has been steadily expanding exports from its US-sanctioned export plants — Arctic LNG 2 and Portovaya — most buyers remain wary of taking restricted shipments out of fear of retaliation from Washington.

topStories.title: Amazon's delivery battle could hit your pocket hard? What you need to know about Mamdani's new bill
topStories.description: Amazon is disputing a New York City bill that could require it to hire thousands of delivery workers, potentially increasing delivery costs and disrupting its current model. The bill, reintroduced by Tiffany Caban, may lead Amazon to relocate its distribution centers outside the city.

dailyNews.description: Polling began at 7 a.m. and will conclude by 5 p.m. across 31,490 polling stations in 35 districts.

dailyNews.description: Hundreds of vehicles have driven through Istanbul to condemn the ‘lawless aggression’ of the US and Israel.

dailyNews.title: Pakistani man pleads guilty to plotting ISIS-style attack in New York: ‘Goal of killing as many Jews as possible’
dailyNews.description: The attack was timed to coincide with the anniversary of the 7 October Hamas attacks. Khan had boasted that his plot could be the largest attack on US soil since 9/11.

dailyNews.description: The US president says Nato "wasn't there when we needed them" during the Iran war.

dailyNews.title: Jim Whittaker dies at 97 — Here's all to know about the 1st American to summit Mount Everest
dailyNews.description: Jim Whittaker is survived by his wife of 52 years, Dianne Roberts; sons Bob, Joss and Leif Whittaker; three grandchildren; and one great-grandchild.

dailyNews.title: Michigan woman arrested for allegedly starving, torturing disabled sister-in-law she locked in basement
dailyNews.description: A Michigan woman was arrested after she allegedly kept her disabled sister-in-law locked in a basement for two years and nearly starved her to death.

dailyNews.title: South Carolina pastor, wife arrested after alleged sexual, physical abuse of foster children
dailyNews.description: A South Carolina pastor and his wife were arrested after a foster child reported that the pastor sexually abused them while at the couple's home.

dailyNews.title: Man charged after allegedly threatening to kill Ohio dad 'in the name of Allah' in terrifying video encounter
dailyNews.description: Anthony Long allegedly told an Ohio father he would kill him "in the name of Allah" in a terrifying encounter caught on camera.

dailyNews.title: Dem lawmaker calls for TSA to bring back shoes-off airport security policy
dailyNews.description: Sen. Tammy Duckworth is calling on the TSA to bring back its controversial policy requiring travelers to remove their shoes before going through security checkpoints at airports.

dailyNews.title: Former Fort Bragg employee charged with leaking classified military information to journalist
dailyNews.description: Courtney Williams is accused of leaking top-secret military tactics to an investigative journalist, according to a federal complaint.

dailyNews.title: 4/8: The Takeout with Major Garrett
dailyNews.description: U.S.-Iran ceasefire in fragile place; NATO chief Mark Rutte meets with President Trump at the White House.

dailyNews.title: Judge postpones termination of TPS for Ethiopians in U.S.
dailyNews.description: It's the latest setback for the Trump administration on the issue, which has sought to terminate the TPS designation for 13 countries as part of the president's crackdown on immigration.

dailyNews.title: Stocks rally and oil prices drop following ceasefire announcement
dailyNews.description: Stocks jumped and oil prices fell on Wednesday following President Trump's announcement of a two-week ceasefire in the war with Iran. CBS News senior business and tech correspondent Jo Ling Kent has more.

dailyNews.title: Ceasefire between Iran and the U.S. fragile amid continued strikes
dailyNews.description: With a fragile ceasefire between the U.S. and Iran in place, Israel launched its biggest attacks yet against Iran-backed Hezbollah in Lebanon. Iran says Lebanon is part of the ceasefire, while the U.S. and Israel say it is not. The White House announced Vice President J.D. Vance will travel to Pakistan for negotiations with Iran. NBC News’ Gabe Gutierrez reports.

dailyNews.title: Oil plunges on Iran ceasefire news, setting the stage for some relief on gas prices
dailyNews.description: Stocks roared higher after President Trump announced a two-week ceasefire with Iran. Oil prices fell sharply, and gas prices are expected to follow.

dailyNews.title: Strikes on Lebanon test U.S.-Israeli relations
dailyNews.description: Israeli strikes on Lebanon have reportedly stalled the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz. NBC News' Matt Bradley joins "Here's the Scoop" to explain how this delay could strain the relationship between President Trump and Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu.

dailyNews.title: White House defends Trump's 'very tough rhetoric' on Iran
dailyNews.description: NBC News’ Gabe Gutierrez questions White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt about President Trump’s Truth Social post threatening to wipe out “a whole civilization” on Tuesday.

dailyNews.title: Trump 'discarded' Iran's original 10-point plan
dailyNews.description: Trump 'discarded' Iran's original 10-point plan

dailyNews.title: Trump says U.S. ready for 'next conquest,' warns military to remain near Iran until 'real agreement' is honored
dailyNews.description: The statement came a day after Washington and Tehran agreed to a two-week ceasefire brokered by Pakistan, which halted six weeks of fighting.

dailyNews.title: 'Poorly run, piece of ice': Trump targets Greenland again as Iran war deepens NATO rift
dailyNews.description: Trump has set his eyes on Greenland again while venting frustration at NATO, as the diplomatic fallout from Iran war exposed rifts in the security alliance.

dailyNews.title: Trump wants Strait of Hormuz open 'without limitation, including tolls' during Iran ceasefire: White House
dailyNews.description: Iran is reportedly planning to demand that shipping firms pay tolls in cryptocurrency to let their oil tankers through the Strait of Hormuz.
